questions.question: What does development mainly refer to?
questions.explanation: Development means improvement in people’s quality of life.

questions.question: Which is a basic goal of development?
questions.explanation: Higher income helps improve living standards.

questions.question: Per capita income is:
questions.explanation: Per capita income means average income per person.

questions.question: Which organization publishes the Human Development Report?
questions.explanation: UNDP publishes the Human Development Report.

questions.question: HDI stands for:
questions.explanation: HDI means Human Development Index.

questions.question: Which is an example of a non-income factor of development?
questions.explanation: Education improves quality of life and is a non-income factor.

questions.question: Life expectancy shows:

options.A: Average years a person is expected to live

questions.explanation: Life expectancy indicates average lifespan.

questions.question: Which country is considered developed?
questions.explanation: Countries with high HDI are considered developed.

questions.question: Development goals differ because:
questions.explanation: Different people have different needs and priorities.

questions.question: Which is a public facility?
questions.explanation: Government hospitals are public facilities.

questions.question: Sustainable development means:
questions.explanation: Sustainable development protects resources for future generations.

questions.question: Which is a renewable resource?
questions.explanation: Sunlight is a renewable resource.

questions.question: Which factor improves health in a country?
questions.explanation: Better hospitals improve health standards.

questions.question: Infant mortality rate refers to:

options.B: Deaths of infants below one year

questions.explanation: Infant mortality rate means deaths of infants below one year.

questions.question: Which of the following is a developed country?
questions.explanation: USA is considered a developed country.

questions.question: Main aim of development is:
questions.explanation: Development focuses on overall better living conditions.

questions.question: Which is an example of natural resource?
questions.explanation: River is a natural resource.

questions.question: Which indicator shows education level?
questions.explanation: Literacy rate measures education level.

questions.question: Which sector provides public facilities?
questions.explanation: Public sector provides public facilities.

questions.question: Higher literacy rate means:
questions.explanation: Higher literacy rate indicates more educated population.

questions.question: Which resource is non-renewable?
questions.explanation: Coal is a non-renewable resource.

questions.question: Development of a country depends on:
questions.explanation: Development depends on income, health, and education.

questions.question: Which country has higher development?
questions.explanation: High HDI indicates higher development.

questions.question: Clean drinking water is an example of:
questions.explanation: Clean drinking water is a public facility.

questions.question: Which is the best measure of development?

options.C: Combination of income, health and education

questions.explanation: Development is best measured using multiple indicators like income, health, and education.
